key_insight: A polygonal cell-based smoothed finite element framework enables nonlinear geotechnical analysis on flexible meshes by combining Wachspress interpolation with boundary-based strain smoothing and incremental elasto-plastic updates.
engineering_relevance: The method is well suited for complex soil-structure problems with staged construction, local failure, and hanging-node quadtree meshes, while improving robustness for practical ABAQUS-based geotechnical simulation.
method: Uses polygonal CS-FEM with Wachspress shape functions
Computes smoothed strains via boundary integration over smoothing subcells
Supports Mohr-Coulomb and Duncan-Chang nonlinear constitutive models in a UEL implementation
evidence_signal: The abstract reports verification on multiple benchmark and engineering cases, including perforated plate, strip footing, core rockfill dam, tunnel excavation, and slope stability, with accurate displacement, stress, plastic strain, bearing capacity, and factor-of-safety predictions.
watch_item: As a user-element implementation, accuracy and stability will depend on careful constitutive integration, boundary quadrature, and handling of complex polygonal/hanging-node meshes.
paper_title: Nonlinear Geotechnical Analysis Using a Polygonal Cell-Based Smoothed Finite Element Framework
